sources.title: Exotic critical states as fractional Fermi seas in the one-dimensional Bose gas
sources.authors: A. Bastianello, Y. Zeng, S. Dhar, Z. Wang, X. Yu, M. Horvath, G. E. Astrakharchik, Y. Guo, H.-C. Nägerl, and M. Landini
sources.usable_claim: Cyclic interaction changes in a one-dimensional Bose gas can be modeled as producing fractional Fermi seas with reduced occupancy, generalized-hydrodynamic structure, Friedel-oscillation signatures, and critical behavior incompatible with a conventional Tomonaga-Luttinger liquid.
sources.boundary: This is an external condensed-matter and quantum-gas result. It supplies comparator mathematics and calibration targets, not an automatic validation of LCR ontology.

sources.title: Realization of fractional Fermi seas
sources.authors: Y. Zeng, A. Bastianello, S. Dhar, Z. Wang, X. Yu, M. Horvath, G. E. Astrakharchik, Y. Guo, H.-C. Nägerl, and M. Landini
sources.venue: arXiv:2602.17657 (2026); experimental realization reported as under review
sources.usable_claim: An excited one-dimensional Bose gas prepared by ramping cycles can realize stable fractional Fermi sea states whose Friedel oscillations act as experimental signatures.
sources.boundary: Use as an external realization and measurement-comparator source until final journal metadata is attached.

sources.title: Quantum mechanics based on real numbers: A consistent description
sources.authors: P. Barrios Hita, A. Trushechkin, H. Kampermann, M. Epping, and D. Bruss
sources.usable_claim: A physically motivated composite-system postulate can construct a real-number quantum formalism reproducing the predictions of complex quantum mechanics for multipartite experiments, making complex numbers a representational convenience within that construction.
sources.boundary: Use as a representation/formalism-comparison source. It does not by itself prove LCR, but it directly supports the paper suite's distinction between addressed state and chosen mathematical label.

FLCR-02.use: Supports the carrier-layer distinction between an addressed physical state and the representational field chosen to describe it.
FLCR-02.boundary: Do not infer that every LCR relabeling is physically equivalent; equivalence requires an explicit construction or adapter.

FLCR-08.use: Strengthens bridge-language around label changes, quotient/equivalence structure, and preserving predictions under a changed formal description.
FLCR-08.boundary: The imported lesson is formal equivalence under stated postulates, not a generic permission to erase phase, locality, or tensor-product constraints.

FLCR-10.use: Provides an external example of a time-windowed preparation protocol where cyclic interaction ramps create a stable excited state with readable downstream signatures.
FLCR-10.boundary: The paper may use this as a comparator for temporal-window and prestate/poststate reasoning, not as proof that LCR generated the state.

FLCR-36.use: Adds fractional occupancy, Friedel-oscillation, generalized-hydrodynamic, and non-TLL critical-phase comparators to the condensed-matter/materials bridge.
FLCR-36.boundary: Quantitative LCR claims must reproduce the observed distributions, correlation functions, or oscillation signatures before being promoted.

FLCR-36.use: Adds an experimental quantum-gas realization route for checking whether LCR/tarpit/crystal tools can reconstruct stable exotic occupancy states from measured signatures.
FLCR-36.boundary: Treat under-review experimental details as provisional until final journal metadata and data products are attached.

FLCR-38.use: Supports the observer/computation argument that a formal runtime can preserve predictions while changing the representation used to carry state.
FLCR-38.boundary: This supports representation independence only inside an explicitly defined formalism; it does not remove the need for LCR observer adapters.

FLCR-39.use: Creates a concrete comparator: LCR condensed-matter claims should be tested against fractional Fermi sea occupancy, Friedel oscillations, generalized hydrodynamics, and TLL-incompatibility claims.
FLCR-39.boundary: Failure to match those external signatures is a falsifier or narrowing condition for relevant LCR material claims.

FLCR-39.use: Creates a representation-equivalence comparator: LCR relabeling claims must specify the postulates and adapters under which predictions are preserved.
FLCR-39.boundary: A relabeling without an equivalence proof remains only notation.

FLCR-40.use: Routes fractional Fermi sea theory into the reconstruction map as a future-drawable external comparator for exotic occupancy and nonstandard critical phases.
FLCR-40.boundary: FLCR-40 lists the route; lower papers or FLCR-41..80 must do the reconstruction work.

FLCR-40.use: Routes real-valued quantum mechanics into the reconstruction map as a major external support for separating physical prediction from representational convenience.
FLCR-40.boundary: FLCR-40 should not cite this as proof of grand unification; it is a map entry and comparator.

boundary_policy: External physics papers may strengthen citation-bound comparators, calibration targets, and falsifiers. They do not promote LCR claims unless a formal adapter, receipt, or reconstruction is added.
